anthracnose.overview: A fungal disease caused by Colletotrichum gloeosporioides. Infected pods develop dark brown to black lesions that spread rapidly, especially during wet seasons. Can cause up to 30–40% crop loss if untreated.
symptoms: Dark brown to black lesions on pod surface
Lesions spread rapidly during wet seasons
Pods may rot completely if untreated
treatments: Remove and destroy infected pods immediately
Harvest ripe pods promptly to reduce spread
prevention: Prune trees regularly to improve air circulation
Remove diseased material from the farm
Apply preventive fungicide sprays before wet season

cssvd.overview: A severe viral disease transmitted by mealybugs. Causes swelling of shoots, root tips, and pods. Infected trees show reduced yield and eventually die. There is no cure — infected trees must be removed.
symptoms: Swelling of young shoots and stems
Pods show chlorotic patches and distortion
treatments: Remove infected trees and surrounding contact trees
Report to local COCOBOD extension officer immediately
Control mealybug vectors with approved insecticides
prevention: Plant CSSVD-tolerant or resistant cocoa varieties
Monitor farm weekly for early symptoms
Report suspected cases to COCOBOD promptly

healthy.overview: A healthy cocoa leaf with no visible signs of disease. Continue good agricultural practices to maintain plant health.
prevention: Prune regularly to improve air circulation
Apply balanced fertilization (N-P-K) based on soil tests
Remove dead or diseased plant material promptly
Harvest ripe pods within 2–3 weeks of ripening
